phases.title: FUNDAMENTAL LINUX
phases.description: Master the basics of Linux command line and system

modules.title: Linux Basic Navigation
modules.description: Learn pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, and basic directory operations

content.intro: Linux navigation is the foundation of everything in cybersecurity. You must be comfortable moving around the filesystem before you can hack anything.
concepts: pwd - Print Working Directory: shows where you are in the filesystem
ls - List: shows files and folders in current directory
cd - Change Directory: move between folders
mkdir - Make Directory: create new folders
rmdir - Remove Directory: delete empty folders

commands.desc: List ALL files including hidden with details

practice: Navigate to /tmp and create a folder named 'myfolder'
Go inside 'myfolder' and create 3 files with touch
List all files with details using ls -la
Go back to your home directory using cd ~
Use pwd to confirm you're in your home directory

modules.title: File Operations
modules.description: Create, copy, move, and delete files

content.intro: Files are everywhere in Linux. Mastering file operations is essential for daily tasks and security work.
concepts: touch - Create empty file or update timestamp
rm - Remove file (careful! no undo in Linux)
-r flag for recursive operations on directories
practice: Create a file named 'notes.txt' with touch
Copy it to 'notes_backup.txt'
Rename 'notes_backup.txt' to 'backup.txt'
Delete the backup file with rm
Create a directory and copy multiple files into it

modules.title: File Permissions
modules.description: Understand and modify file permissions (chmod, chown)

content.intro: Linux permissions control who can read, write, and execute files. Critical for system security.
concepts: Owner, Group, Others - three permission layers
chown - Change file owner and group
Sticky bit, SUID, SGID special permissions
practice: Create a script.sh and make it executable
Change permissions to 600 (owner read+write only)
View the permission changes with ls -la
Change ownership of a file (requires sudo)
Understand the difference between 755 and 777

modules.title: User Management
modules.description: Manage users, groups, and sudo access

content.intro: User management is key for system administration and security. Understanding users helps you escalate privileges.

commands.desc: Create new user with home dir

practice: Check who you are with whoami and id
Switch to root with sudo su and back with exit
Create a new user 'testuser'
Add testuser to the sudo group
View the /etc/passwd file

content.intro: Processes are running programs. Managing them is crucial for security and system administration.
concepts: kill - Terminate a process with signals
jobs - Background jobs in current shell
practice: Run 'top' and identify the top 3 processes by CPU
Find all processes with 'ssh' in the name
Start a background process with & and bring it back
Kill a process using kill -9
Use ps to find your own shell process

modules.title: Text Processing
modules.description: grep, sed, awk, and text manipulation

content.intro: Text processing is essential for log analysis, parsing outputs, and automation in cybersecurity.
concepts: grep - Search text patterns using regex
sed - Stream editor for filtering and transforming
awk - Pattern scanning and processing language
practice: Search for 'root' in /etc/passwd
Count how many users are in the system
Find all .conf files in /etc/ using grep
Use grep with regex to find IP addresses
Chain grep with other commands using pipe

content.intro: Shell scripting automates tasks and is fundamental for penetration testing and system administration.
concepts: #!/bin/bash - Shebang line tells system which interpreter
practice: Create a script that prints your name and today's date
Write a script that counts files in current directory
Create a script that asks for user input and responds
Write a script with if/else that checks if a file exists
Create a loop that prints numbers 1-10

modules.title: Package Management
modules.description: Install, update, and remove software packages

content.intro: Package management is how you install tools and software on Linux. Essential for setting up your hacking lab.
practice: Update your system with sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
Install nmap if not already installed
Check nmap version with nmap --version
Search for and install htop
Remove a package and verify it's gone

content.intro: IP addresses are like home addresses for devices on a network. Understanding them is crucial for network security.
practice: Find your IP address using ip a
Ping google.com and observe response time
Understand the difference between 192.168.x.x and public IPs
Calculate subnet for a /24 network
Find your default gateway

content.intro: DNS translates human-readable domain names to IP addresses. Understanding DNS is key for reconnaissance.
concepts: DNS record types: A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, TXT
practice: Lookup the IP of google.com using dig
Add a custom entry to /etc/hosts
Query different DNS record types (MX, TXT, CNAME)
Find the authoritative nameserver for a domain
Use nslookup to find subdomains

content.intro: Ports are like doors to a computer. Each service uses specific ports. Port scanning is fundamental to recon.
concepts: UDP = fast but unreliable (DNS, gaming, VoIP)
practice: List all open ports on your machine
Identify which ports SSH and HTTP use
Scan localhost with nmap
Use netstat to find which process uses port 443
Understand the difference between TCP and UDP

content.intro: These tools help diagnose and troubleshoot network issues. Essential for both attack and defense.
concepts: traceroute - Trace packet path through networks
practice: Ping google.com 10 times and note the response time
Trace the route to a remote server
Download a file with wget
Use curl to check HTTP headers of a website
Find your public IP using curl

content.intro: Wireshark is a network protocol analyzer for deep packet inspection. Essential for understanding network attacks.
practice: Capture 10 packets with tcpdump
Filter for HTTP traffic only
Analyze a simple packet capture
Follow a TCP stream in Wireshark
Identify DNS queries in a capture

content.intro: The CIA Triad is the foundation of information security. Every security decision relates to these three principles.
concepts: Confidentiality - Only authorized access to data
Integrity - Data is accurate and unmodified
Balancing CIA - tradeoffs between security and usability
practice: Identify CIA in a real-world scenario (e.g., online banking)
Analyze a data breach using CIA framework
Design security controls for each CIA component
List how encryption protects confidentiality
Explain how checksums protect integrity

modules.title: Authentication & Authorization
modules.description: Who are you and what can you do?

content.intro: Authentication verifies identity, authorization determines permissions. Both are essential for security.
practice: Compare different authentication methods
Set up SSH key-based authentication
Analyze Linux permissions as access control
Implement MFA on a test account
Review /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow

content.intro: Cryptography protects data through mathematical techniques. Understanding it is crucial for security professionals.
concepts: Key exchange and perfect forward secrecy
practice: Generate hash of a string using sha256sum
Compare MD5 and SHA256 outputs for same input
Encrypt and decrypt a file with openssl
Create a self-signed certificate with openssl
Understand why MD5 is considered insecure

modules.title: Common Attacks
modules.description: SQL injection, XSS, phishing, and more

content.intro: Understanding attacks is essential for defense. Learn how attackers think to protect better.
concepts: SQL Injection (SQLi) - injecting SQL through input
Brute Force attacks - trying all combinations
practice: Identify SQL injection in sample vulnerable code
Recognize phishing email patterns
Test for reflected XSS in a web form
Set up a basic brute force attack in lab
Explain how MITM attacks work

content.intro: Recon is the first and most important phase of penetration testing. The more you know, the easier the attack.
practice: Perform WHOIS lookup on a domain
Use Google dorking to find sensitive files
Gather emails and subdomains for a target
Create a recon report for a test target
Use OSINT to find information about a company

content.intro: Nmap is THE most important tool for network scanning. Mastering it is essential for any penetration tester.
practice: Scan localhost with different options
Scan for all open ports on a target
Identify running services and versions
Use NSE scripts to find vulnerabilities
Compare SYN scan vs TCP connect scan

content.intro: Metasploit is the world's most used penetration testing framework. It makes exploitation easier and more organized.
practice: Search for vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor exploit
Configure and run an exploit against Metasploitable
Navigate Meterpreter after successful exploit
Use auxiliary modules for scanning
Practice with different payload types

content.intro: Web applications are the most common attack surface. Understanding web vulnerabilities is crucial.
concepts: Local File Inclusion (LFI) / Remote File Inclusion (RFI)
practice: Test a DVWA instance for SQL injection
Find hidden directories with dirb/gobuster
Exploit a basic XSS vulnerability
Practice LFI/RFI on a vulnerable app
Write a basic vulnerability report

content.intro: After getting initial access, you need to escalate privileges to fully compromise the system.
practice: Find SUID binaries and research exploits
Check for sudo misconfigurations
Exploit a SUID binary for privilege escalation
Use LinPEAS to enumerate a system
Practice on HackTheBox or TryHackMe machines

phases.title: DEFENSIVE SECURITY
phases.description: SOC, Blue Team, and incident response

content.intro: Logs are the eyes and ears of security monitoring. Learning to read them is essential for detection.
practice: Find failed SSH login attempts in auth.log
Analyze web server access logs for patterns
Create a simple log monitoring script
Identify suspicious activity in logs
Use awk to parse log files

modules.title: Incident Response
modules.description: How to respond to security incidents

content.intro: Incident response is a structured approach to handling security breaches. Preparation is key.
practice: Create an incident response checklist
Analyze a compromised system scenario
Document findings and recommendations
Practice chain of custody for evidence
Create an incident response plan for a company

content.intro: SIEM tools collect and analyze security data in real-time. Essential for SOC analysts.
practice: Set up a basic SIEM in a lab environment
Create alert rules for suspicious activity
Build a security dashboard
Perform threat hunting with queries
Analyze false positives and tune alerts

content.intro: Firewalls and IDS/IPS are the first line of defense. Understanding them helps both attack and defense.
practice: Configure UFW to allow only SSH and HTTP
Write basic Snort rules
Analyze firewall logs
Set up a basic IDS in lab environment
Test rules against known attack patterns

content.intro: Malware analysis helps understand threats and develop countermeasures. Essential for advanced defense.
practice: Analyze a benign executable with static tools
Extract strings and find indicators
Use a sandbox for behavioral analysis
Write basic YARA rules
Document malware capabilities

content.intro: Burp Suite is the industry standard for web application security testing. Master it to test web apps effectively.
practice: Configure browser to use Burp proxy
Intercept and modify HTTP requests
Use Repeater to test input validation
Perform a basic SQL injection with Intruder
Analyze a web application's authentication

content.intro: Practice makes perfect. Use vulnerable web apps to hone your web exploitation skills safely.
practice: Set up and exploit DVWA vulnerabilities
Complete WebGoat lessons
Solve HackTheBox web challenges
Document your findings professionally
Practice responsible reporting

modules.title: API Security
modules.description: REST and GraphQL API security testing

content.intro: APIs are everywhere. Securing them is critical as they're often the backbone of modern applications.
practice: Test API authentication mechanisms
Find and exploit IDOR vulnerabilities
Test for mass assignment
Implement proper API security controls
Analyze API documentation for weaknesses

content.intro: As AI becomes prevalent, securing AI systems and using AI for security becomes critical.
practice: Understand adversarial examples conceptually
Analyze AI model vulnerabilities
Explore defensive techniques
Research recent AI security incidents
Discuss ethical implications of AI in security

modules.title: AI-Powered Security Tools
modules.description: Using AI for cybersecurity defense and offense

content.intro: AI is revolutionizing cybersecurity - both for attackers and defenders. Learn to leverage AI tools.
practice: Set up a basic ML-based IDS
Analyze network traffic with ML models
Test AI-powered security tools
Build a simple threat detection model
Evaluate AI security tool effectiveness

content.intro: This module helps you plan your cybersecurity career and prepare for industry certifications.

commands.cmd: Contribute to open source security tools

practice: Create a cybersecurity career plan
Set up a complete home lab
Start a bug bounty hunting journey
Build a professional portfolio
Join cybersecurity communities and competitions
